GraceChangeDesignContext.Rationale: Keeping provider.openai preserves OpenCode built-in OAuth and proxy compatibility. Codex-specific alias names make the smaller subscription contract explicit, while fully specified limits prevent models.dev API metadata from silently restoring a 922000-token input budget.

Alternatives.Alternative: A custom openai-codex-subscription provider was rejected because it would not participate in OpenCode's built-in OpenAI OAuth integration and would require separate credentials and provider metadata.
Keeping generic vv-gpt-* names was rejected because those names conceal whether their limits represent direct API or Codex subscription behavior.
Changing built-in openai/gpt-* metadata was rejected because standard models.dev entries correctly represent direct API capabilities.

DeferredDecisions.Decision: A separately curated direct-API vv-openai-api preset can be introduced later if real demand appears.
Legacy alias cleanup may be added later as an explicit opt-in migration rather than an automatic destructive write.

Scenarios.Scenario: A CLIProxyAPI user selects openai/vv-codex-gpt-5.6-sol-xhigh; OpenCode compacts near the 352000 usable threshold instead of waiting near 902000 tokens.
A native OpenCode OAuth user selects the same alias and receives the same conservative subscription limits under provider.openai.
A direct API user selects a built-in openai/gpt-* model and retains full models.dev API limits.
